2605.04941.title: UFAL-CUNI at SemEval-2026 Task 11: An Efficient Modular Neuro-symbolic Method for Syllogistic Reasoning
2605.04941.paper_summary: This paper describes our system submitted to SemEval-2026 Task 11: Disentangling Content and Formal Reasoning in Large Language Models. We present an efficient modular neuro-symbolic approach, combining a symbolic prover with small reasoning LLMs (4B parameters). The system consists of an LLM-based parser that translates natural language syllogisms to a first-order logic (FOL) representation, an automated theorem prover, and two optional modules: machine translation for multilingual inputs and a symbolic retrieval component for the identification of relevant premises. The system achieves competitive accuracy and relatively low content effect on most subtasks. Our ablations show that this approach outperforms LLM-based zero-shot baselines in this parameter size range, but also reveal limited multilingual capabilities of small LLMs. Finally, we include a discussion of the task's main ranking metric and analyze its limitations.
2605.04941.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文描述了我們提交給 SemEval-2026 第 11 任務的系統：在大型語言模型中解開內容與形式推理。<lb/>我們提出了一種高效的模組化神經符號方法，結合了符號證明器和小型推理 LLM（4B 參數）。<lb/>該系統由一個基於 LLM 的解析器組成，將自然語言三段論翻譯為一階邏輯（FOL）表示，還包括一個自動定理證明器和兩個可選模組：用於多語言輸入的機器翻譯和一個符號檢索組件，用於識別相關前提。<lb/>該系統在大多數子任務中達到了具有競爭力的準確性和相對較低的內容影響。<lb/>我們的消融實驗顯示，這種方法在這個參數範圍內超越了基於 LLM 的零-shot 基準，但也揭示了小型 LLM 的多語言能力有限。<lb/>最後，我們包括了對任務主要排名指標的討論並分析其局限性。
2605.04941.author: Ivan Kartáč et.al.
2605.04941.authors: Ivan Kartáč, Kristýna Onderková, Jan Bronec, Zdeněk Kasner, Mateusz Lango, Ondřej Dušek
